subject: UX & Research
title: The Vocabulary Problem
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Vocabulary Problem usable.

hook.text: Users call the same thing by 20 different names. A 'shopping cart' is also a 'basket,' 'bag,' and 'trolley.'

buildup.text: Furnas et al. found in 1987 that two people use the same word for the same thing only 20% of the time.

discovery.text: This means your label will miss 80% of users' first guess. Search must handle synonyms to bridge the vocabulary gap.

twist.text: Within one company, teams call features different names. Users encounter inconsistency across the product.

climax.text: Build a controlled vocabulary: one official term per concept, with synonyms mapped for search and help content.

punchline.text: Users won't guess your word. Map their synonyms to your terms.

quiz.question: What did Furnas's research reveal about how people name things?
options: Two people use the same word for the same thing only 20% of the time
Most people use identical vocabulary for common objects
Technical users and non-technical users use the same words
